programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast by Reform UK
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by Reform UK for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Vernon Kay bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's search continues, two Walford residents face a race against time, and Nigel receives some visitors.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: It's a family affair as Steve Fletcher fixes a model watermill while son Fred brings a cherished clock back to life. The team also fix a stunning Notting Hill Carnival headpiece.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: A fresh set of hopeful home cooks serve up their signature dishes and precision cooking in a bid to impress the MasterChef judges and get through to the quarter-finals.

programme.title: BBC News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Champions League Highlights
programme.desc: Highlights from the first-leg matches in the semi-finals of the UEFA Champions League as Paris Saint-Germain play Bayern Munich and Atletico Madrid tackle Arsenal.

programme.title: Denmark
programme.desc: An unhappy Herb hears being held in a Danish jail is more comfy than his life in Wales. Contains very strong language.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Scams
programme.desc: In a special scams episode, the team meets the 20-year-old who was waiting to receive a tax rebate from HMRC, only to discover someone else had cashed the cheque weeks earlier.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is in Derby to see a plot of land.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Since Barbie's debut in 1959, more than a billion dolls have been sold in a variety of models. Natasha meets a collector who owns some very special examples.

programme.title: BBC News at One including BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Eastbourne, siblings David and Theresa battle to raise money for DKMS, gambling on designer fashion, vintage finds and bold upcycled pieces.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo and his team are in South Uist in the Outer Hebrides, turning an unloved empty garage into a fully functioning shop for crofters DJ and Lindsay.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A young couple want to swap their London flat for something bigger in the East Midlands, where Nicki Chapman opens the front doors to a lovely selection of homes with lovely gardens.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Can pyrographer Chantal finally make a full-time living from her craft, and will Claire be able to build a stained-glass business to replace her 33-year sugar craft career.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Family of four Conn, Paula, Brian and Sophia take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they must cross the Bridge, stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz where contestants try to score as few points as possible by coming up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast by the Green Party
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Green Party for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Albert Square comes together to celebrate a friend, Phil battles his inner demons, and Max and Cindy's romance causes friction.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams discover their next checkpoint lies in Zaamin National Park, Uzbekistan.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The second quarter-final is here, and the six best cooks of the week return to prove they have more culinary skill in the tank, starting with an invention test.

programme.title: BBC News and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate.

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Lighthearted discussion of the day's news.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: The passengers told to wait in departures for a delayed flight that took off without them.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A tale of two very different million-pound-plus projects, one in central London, the other in Anglesey. Martin's also in Bristol to see a two-bedroom flat.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: It's a sibling showdown in West Yorkshire with Danny Sebastian.

programme.title: BBC News at One including BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Rock 'n' roll reaches Kembleford when a group of bikers cause outrage and excitement in equal measure.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown is on hand to help a couple of newlyweds looking to escape South London and set down roots in the stunning countryside of Cumbria.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Newly trained blacksmith Clare needs to start making money to buy her first home with her partner, and baker Gavin wants to use his cakes to help him move out of his mum's house.

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Faye Brookes, Richard Blackwood, Natalie Anderson and John Partridge take on the Bridge.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz where contestants try to score as few points as possible by coming up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcast by the Liberal Democrats
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Liberal Democrats for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Clara Amfo bring you the stories that matter from across the country.
